description: Transnational Sunni Islamist organization founded in Egypt in 1928 by Hassan al-Banna. Combines political activism with Islamic charity work, aiming to establish a state ruled by Sharia under a caliphate. It gained legalization and political power after the Arab Spring but was banned and declared a terrorist organization in Egypt following the 2013 coup. The group has influenced various Islamist movements and remains active in multiple countries.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Muslim Brotherhood reached Algeria during the later years of the French colonial presence ... the Muslim Brotherhood formed the Movement of Society for Peace (MSP, previously known as Hamas)
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood reached Algeria and formed the Movement of Society for Peace, a legal political party.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 16 January 2026, Argentina designated the Egyptian, Jordanian, and Lebanese branches of the Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organizations.
country_links.context: Argentina designated branches of the Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organizations.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Muslim Brotherhood had opened an office in Western Sydney
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood opened an office in Western Sydney, Australia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Following parliamentary elections in 2002, Al Menbar became the largest joint party with eight seats in the forty-seat Chamber of Deputies .
country_links.context: Al Menbar, the largest joint party in Bahrain's Chamber of Deputies, is a Brotherhood affiliate.

country_links.evidence_quote: The brotherhood's build-up in France started with Union des organisations islamiques en France (UOIF)... By 2020, there were 147 mosques and 18 Islamic schools associated with the brotherhood.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood built up its presence in France through the Union des organisations islamiques en France (UOIF), which has mosques and schools.

country_links.evidence_quote: Ramadan built a major center for the Brotherhood centered on a mosque in Munich, which became 'a refuge for the beleaguered group during its decades in the wilderness'.
country_links.context: Said Ramadan built a major center for the Brotherhood centered on a mosque in Munich.

country_links.evidence_quote: Iranian Call and Reform Organization , a Sunni Islamist group active in Iran, predominantly among Kurds, has been described as an organization 'that belongs to the Muslim Brotherhood'
country_links.context: The Iranian Call and Reform Organization, a Sunni Islamist group active in Iran, has been described as belonging to the Muslim Brotherhood.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Iraqi Islamic Party was formed in 1960 as the Iraqi branch of the Brotherhood
country_links.context: The Iraqi Islamic Party was formed in 1960 as the Iraqi branch of the Brotherhood.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 9 November 1945 the Association of the Muslim Brotherhood (Jam'iyat al-Ikhwan al-Muslimin) was officially registered and Abu Qura became its first General Supervisor.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood in Jordan was officially registered in 1945, operates as a political and social organization, and has been involved in protests and parliamentary politics.

country_links.evidence_quote: Kazakhstan – 15 March 2005
country_links.context: Kazakhstan listed the Muslim Brotherhood as a terrorist organization.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September 2025, Kenya designated the Muslim Brotherhood as a terrorist organization.
country_links.context: Kenya designated the Muslim Brotherhood as a terrorist organization.

country_links.evidence_quote: Egyptian Brethren came to Kuwait in the 1950s as refugees from Arab nationalism and integrated into the education ministry and other parts of the state.
country_links.context: Egyptian Muslim Brotherhood members came to Kuwait in the 1950s, and the group's charity and political arms operate there, with members elected to parliament.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Lebanese branch, known as al-Jamaa al-Islamiyah , was designated both as a Foreign Terrorist Organization and a Specially Designated Global Terrorist entity.
country_links.context: The Lebanese branch of the Muslim Brotherhood (al-Jamaa al-Islamiyah) was designated as a terrorist organization by the U.S. and Argentina.

country_links.evidence_quote: A group of the Muslim Brotherhood came to the Libyan kingdom in the 1950s as refugees ... the Brotherhood announced that it would form the Justice and Construction Party (JCP)
country_links.context: A group of the Muslim Brotherhood came to Libya and later formed the Justice and Construction Party.

country_links.evidence_quote: Angkatan Belia Islam Malaysia in Malaysia is close to the Brotherhood.
country_links.context: Angkatan Belia Islam Malaysia in Malaysia is close to the Muslim Brotherhood.

country_links.evidence_quote: Mauritania's first Islamist organisation, known as Jemaa Islamiyya (Islamic Association) was formed by Mauritanians sympathetic to the Muslim Brotherhood.
country_links.context: Mauritania's first Islamist organisation was formed by Mauritanians sympathetic to the Muslim Brotherhood.

country_links.evidence_quote: Beyond that the Brotherhood sponsors national organizations in countries like Tunisia ( Ennahda Movement ), Morocco (Justice and Charity party), Algeria ( Movement of Society for Peace ).
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood sponsors the Justice and Charity party in Morocco.

country_links.evidence_quote: 'Abd al-Rahman al-Banna, the brother of the Muslim Brotherhood founder Hasan al-Banna, went to Mandatory Palestine and established the Muslim Brotherhood there in 1935.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood established itself in Mandatory Palestine in 1935 and later operated in the West Bank and Gaza, building social institutions and eventually founding Hamas.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1999 the Muslim Brotherhood was disbanded in Qatar. The country's longstanding support for the group has been often explained as determined by a strategic calculus...
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood was disbanded in Qatar in 1999, but the country has been a patron, hosting influential figures like Yusuf al-Qaradawi and providing media support via Al-Jazeera.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Muslim Brotherhood coordinated the creation of an Islamic organisation called The Supreme Military Majlis ul-Shura of the United Forces of Caucasian Mujahedeen ... an organisation that committed multiple terror-attack acts in Russia
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood coordinated the creation of an Islamic organization that committed terror attacks in Russia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Brotherhood first had an impact inside Saudi Arabia in 1954 when thousands of Egyptian Brethren sought to escape president Gamal Abdel Nasser's clampdown...
country_links.context: Thousands of Egyptian Muslim Brotherhood members fled to Saudi Arabia in 1954 and became entrenched in the education system and government ministries, though without formal organizational presence.

country_links.evidence_quote: Al-Islah has been described as ... which 'operates primarily in Mogadishu'
country_links.context: Al-Islah, described as a nonviolent Islamic movement, operates primarily in Mogadishu and is linked to the Muslim Brotherhood.

country_links.evidence_quote: An offshoot of the Sudanese branch of the Muslim Brotherhood, the Islamic Charter Front grew during the 1960 ... The Muslim Brotherhood/NIF's main objective in Sudan was to Islamize the society
country_links.context: An offshoot of the Sudanese branch of the Muslim Brotherhood, the National Islamic Front, took power and implemented Islamic law.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Muslim Brotherhood in Syria was founded in the 1930s ... It played a major role in the mainly Sunni-based movement that opposed the secularist, pan-Arabist Ba'ath Party.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood in Syria was founded and operated there, playing a major role in the opposition.

country_links.evidence_quote: Tajikistan – 30 March 2006
country_links.context: Tajikistan listed the Muslim Brotherhood as a terrorist organization.

country_links.evidence_quote: Beyond that the Brotherhood sponsors national organizations in countries like Tunisia ( Ennahda Movement ), Morocco (Justice and Charity party), Algeria ( Movement of Society for Peace ).
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood sponsors the Ennahda Movement in Tunisia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Mekameleen TV , a Turkey-based free-to-air satellite television channel run by exiled Brotherhood supporters, mourned his death and claimed it was 'martyrdom'.
country_links.context: Mekameleen TV, a Turkey-based free-to-air satellite television channel run by exiled Brotherhood supporters, mourned his death.

country_links.evidence_quote: Muslim Brotherhood presence in the United Arab Emirates began with the formation of the Al Islah group in the United Arab Emirates in 1974 ... Al Islah in the UAE has openly stated that it shares ideology with the Muslim Brotherhood in Egypt.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood has a presence in the UAE through the Al Islah group, which shares its ideology.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Muslim Brotherhood uses London as an administration base.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood uses London as an administration base and has established affiliated organizations in the UK.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 13 January 2026, the United States government designated three national branches of the Muslim Brotherhood as terrorist organizations under U.S. counterterrorism laws.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood's branches were designated as terrorist organizations by the U.S. government, and the organization had diplomatic channels with the U.S.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Muslim Brothers fought with North Yemen in the NDF rebellion as Islamic Front.
country_links.context: The Muslim Brotherhood fought with North Yemen in the NDF rebellion as Islamic Front.
